digest.component_or_area: PyPI release workflow + GitHub environment IaC
propositions: The only publish gate checks for a v* tag push but never verifies the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main, allowing an arbitrary tagged commit to publish.

Version values across pyproject.toml, server.json top-level, and server.json packages[].version are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking disagreeing versions across PyPI/GitHub-Release/MCP channels.
The release should be re-anchored to trigger via workflow_dispatch from main, guaranteeing reviewed source by construction and replacing the arbitrary-tag publish path.
A pre-build job must assert exact version equality across the input and all manifests, plus a merge-base ancestry check, failing before build on mismatch.
Publication should go directly via OIDC PyPA Trusted Publishing with no intermediate tag relay, creating the v* tag and GitHub Release only after a successful publish as record-only output.

The pypi environment protection (required reviewers + main-only deployment-branch policy) must be codified in terraform so it is code-reviewed and drift-detected rather than an un-versioned console setting.
